main.title: Could Aliens Actually Reach Us?
main.description:
The odds say alien life is probably out there. But could they ever actually get HERE? The honest answer hides one of the most staggering facts about the universe.

What you'll learn:
- Why "the nearest star" is hiding something enormous
- The number that makes our fastest spacecraft look like it's standing still
- The one physics trick that could cross the gulf (and its one fatal problem)
- The lunchtime question from 1950 that still haunts science

Do you think they're out there? Tell us below.

SOURCES
- Proxima Centauri ~4.24 light-years; light ~186,000 mi/s; nothing with mass reaches light speed. (NASA.)
- Voyager-speed craft ~77,000 years to Proxima; Voyager 1 ~1 light-day out after ~50 years. (NASA.)
- Alcubierre warp drive needs negative-energy matter not known to exist. (Wikipedia.)
- Breakthrough Starshot (~20% c, ~20 years; stalled). (Scientific American.)
- Fermi paradox, 1950. (SETI Institute; Planetary Society.)

This video uses AI-assisted narration and illustrations.

#aliens #space #fermiparadox #science #wonder
